search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae dizziness telehealth history red flags investigations referral
site:nice.org.uk NG127 dizziness vertigo adults neurological referral
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic benign positional vertigo, vestibular, cardiac, anxiety, or neurological diagnosis
HINTS or Hallpike implied without trained clinician performance
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Dizziness is a symptom with multiple causes.
Special examinations must not be inferred.
No test, treatment, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: No legacy item in gp-dizziness-in-gp remains source-supported after conservative correction; item-specific population applicability was not independently established without new research.
setting_applicability: No legacy item in gp-dizziness-in-gp remains source-supported after conservative correction; transfer from the recorded source setting to the exact workflow item was not independently established.
UAE_applicability: insufficient_evidence for every removed gp-dizziness-in-gp legacy-item mapping; UAE applicability requires independent item-level review before any mapping is reinstated.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA history section supports symptom description, timing, triggers, concurrent symptoms, age, conditions, functional impact, and duration without assigning a dizziness syndrome.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies symptom description, timing, triggers, concurrent symptoms, age, and pre-existing conditions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports clinician-assessed cardiac, neurological, trauma, intoxication, visual, consciousness, and severe-headache concerns plus available examination without asserting absence.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists cardiac, neurologic, trauma, intoxication, visual, consciousness, and severe-headache concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation section supports recording tests already reviewed when clinically selected from history and examination; it does not generate a test.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: States that tests depend on history and examination and should be documented only when clinically deemed necessary.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact referral section supports clinician-entered safety-net, referral status, and follow-up without assigning urgency automatically.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies emergency and specialist referral features for dizziness.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult neurological section was reviewed for focal-neurological, hearing, acute vestibular, positional, trained-exam, imaging, and referral qualifiers; no specific unconfirmed finding was mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports focal neurological and hearing red flags, acute vestibular syndrome, HINTS by trained clinicians, neuroimaging referral, and Hallpike assessment for positional vertigo.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact syndrome, observations, orthostatic measurements, hearing, gait, neurological examination, cause, diagnosis, tests, medicine, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
